title: Trailing version numbers in URLs detected as files instead of directories
author: timriley
body:
We’re using Parklife to generate the in-development site for [Hanakai](https://hanakai.org) ([source code here](https://github.com/hanakai-rb/site)). Parklife is great! Thank you for making it :)

We have version numbers in the URLs for our guides. For some of those guides, the version numbers come right at the end, e.g. `/learn/dry/dry-types/v1.8`.

Out of the box, Parklife handled those URLs in an unexpected (but understandable) way. It considered the ".8" (in the URL above) to be like a file extname. So it would create a "v1.8" file, rather than creating a "v1.8" directory, which meant it would crash when it tried to write the file for one of the sub-pages of such a guide (e.g. `/learn/dry/dry-types/v1.8/getting-started`).

[We had to patch it](https://github.com/hanakai-rb/site/blob/fd6e078314e05814ce4214baf1b7054cdbde46f5/Parkfile#L5-L26) to make it work for us:

```ruby
# Patch Parklife so it doesn't consider URLs ending with e.g. "v2.0" as having an extname
require "parklife/utils"
Parklife::Utils.class_eval do
  def build_path_for(path, index: true)
    path = path.gsub(/^\/|\/$/, "")

    # This `if` line is what we've modified. Previously it was:
    #
    # if File.extname(path).empty?
    if (extname = File.extname(path)) && (extname.match?(/\.\d+$/) || extname.empty?)
      if path.empty?
        "index.html"
      elsif index
        File.join(path, "index.html")
      else
        "#{path}.html"
      end
    else
      path
    end
  end
end
```

This patch has been working fine for us for the entire time we've been building this site, and I'm happy to carry it into the future, but I did want to let you know about this use case, in case you had ideas for how Parklife might better support it.

Thank you!

title: Unexpected `302 response from path` and `Parklife::HttpError` on build
author: bkjohnson
body:
I'm on parklife version 0.7.0 and rails 8.0.2. I ran `bundle exec parklife init` successfully, and then modified the generated config so that the routes block looks similar to this:

```ruby
Parklife.application.routes do
  # root crawl: true

  get example_model_path(ExampleModel.first)
end
```

I can run `bundle exec parklife routes` without any errors and I see the expected route in the output, including the slug that's coming from ActiveRecord. However, when I try to run `bundle exec parklife build` I get this:

```
bundler: failed to load command: parklife (/home/brooks/.rbenv/versions/3.4.3/bin/parklife)
/home/brooks/.rbenv/versions/3.4.3/lib/ruby/gems/3.4.0/gems/parklife-0.7.0/lib/parklife/crawler.rb:64:in 'Parklife::Crawler#process_route': 302 response from path
```

I thought it was strange that the first line says that parklife isn't found even though the error below clearly comes from parklife. I generated a binstub thinking that that was my problem, but I still have the error.

I even set `config.base` to be the URL that my local rails server is serving traffic on but that didn't seem to impact things. The server is up and I'm even able to hit the endpoint with `curl`, so I'm not sure why parklife can't seem to find it.

title: Improve the HTTP redirect error message
author: benpickles
body: The error message `301 response from path "/" (Parklife::HTTPError)` is somewhat helpful but could be improved. It would be useful to include the redirect location and perhaps detect some particular scenarios such as redirecting to a different scheme.

title: Improve out-of-the-box Rails production build experience
author: benpickles
body:
At the moment with a freshly-generated Rails (8) app and the default parklife/rails settings running `bin/static-build` will result in the cryptic error message `301 response from path "/" (Parklife::HTTPError)`. This is because `force_ssl = true` is enabled in production since Rails 7.1.

I think a couple of things could address this:

1. Improve the Parklife error message, detect and alert redirecting to a different scheme, etc.
2. Have the default parklife/rails integration honour the `force_ssl` setting.

title: 403 error with Sinatra 4.1
author: benpickles
body: Sinatra 4.1 added HostAuthorization rack-protection middleware which causes a 403 error when hit via Parklife. The workaround/fix is simple, set `APP_ENV=production` when running `parklife build` but perhaps it's something Parklife should take care of it like it does with the Rails equivalent?

title: Before- and after-build (or around-) callbacks
author: bensheldon
body:
When I converted my blog over to Parklife, I wanted `parklife build` to produce the finished artifact. I hacked it in by extending the existing `build` method:

https://github.com/bensheldon/island94-jekyll/blob/df433fcdc9ae8bc0ab335d669d002d909a2a02ce/Parkfile#L20-L33


I figure it would be nice to have some simple callbacks like:

```ruby
Parklife.application.before_build do
  Rails.application.load_tasks
  Rake::Task['assets:precompile'].invoke
end

Parklife.application.after_build do
  FileUtils.cp_r(Rails.application.root.join("public/assets"), Rails.application.root.join("build/assets"))
end

Parklife.application.around_build do |build|
  start = Time.now
  build.call
  puts "Finished in #{Time.now - start} seconds"
end
```

I can make a PR if that's ok?

title: Only scan for links on html/textual data
author: bensheldon
body:
Thanks for this gem 🙇🏻

I noticed that the crawler will attempt to scan _everything_ for links, including images:

https://github.com/benpickles/parklife/blob/0d809fc7b70371df3ab46996d064395001f316d2/lib/parklife/crawler.rb#L72

I noticed this because for some strange reason I have a PNG on my blog that scans positively for a blank URI, which then raises a `bad URI(is not URI?): nil (URI::InvalidURIError)` 

This is the image: https://island94.org/uploads/2009-09-13-My-featured-dead-cockroach/wired-cockroach.png

title: Rails / ActiveStorage Crawling Strategy
author: dramalho
body: Unsure if this anyone has raised this before (or even encountered) but I'm trying to figure out if I can replace Jekyll with a very lean rails app to run my blog locally and then use parklife to generate a static build . All good so far - base url, assets, etc - but I've now hit an ActiveStorage issue where I'm taking advantage of the thing to preprocess images (webp / srcset). i.e. on my view I'm using something like ```ruby <%= picture_tag do %> <%= tag(:source, srcset: image.representation(:webp).processed.url) %> <%= tag(:source, srcset: image.representation(:thumb).processed.url) %> <%= tag(:source, srcset: image.representation(:large).processed.url) %> <%= image_tag(image.representation(:medium).processed.url) %> <% end %> ``` which generates ```html ``` Running the build script looks ok too, it's generating urls using the `--base` I need *BUT* it won't actually crawl the images and so they never end up in the `build` folder . I'm unsure how the crawling process work so I'm unsure if it's a problem with not gathering those urls (some are inside a `source` tag , that might be why) or something else. Regular assets are handled by `assets:precompile` + `cp` but this is a slightly different issue and I'm wondering if you have some words of wisdom here :) Thanks

title: 301 when force_ssl = true
author: Spone
body:
I was trying out parklife in a Rails project where `config/production.rb` contains:

    config.force_ssl = true

In this case, I got:

    301 response from path "/" (Parklife::HTTPError)

When changing the config option to `false`, I was able to run `static-build`.

Do you think there's a way to prevent this? (or maybe just add a notice in the README)

title: Idea: using sitemap for crawling
author: Spone
body:
Hi! I'm super excited about this project, since it's pretty similar to an idea I was keeping in the back of my mind. So I'm delighted to find an existing gem that implements it!

One idea I had was using the sitemap as a starting point for crawling the site, since sitemaps are usually a pretty comprehensive list of existing URLs.

What are your thoughts on this?

Related:
- https://github.com/postmodern/spidr/issues/19
- https://github.com/postmodern/spidr/pull/69
- https://github.com/benbalter/sitemap-parser

title: 404 response from path index.html - Using Rails
author: damuz91
body:
Good day!
Today I am playing around with Parklife.
I just picked up a sample rails app i got published on github: https://github.com/damuz91/mi-bodega-rails
Then i have added `parklife` to the Gemfile and tried to follow the step by step from the Readme.
My `Parklife` file looks like this:
```
require_relative 'config/environment'
require 'parklife/rails'

Parklife.application.configure do |config|
end

Parklife.application.routes do
  root crawl: true
  get products_path
end
```

And i have included in my `development.rb` file:
```
Rails.application.config.hosts = nil
```

But when running `parklife build` I get the following error:
```
..Traceback (most recent call last):
        12: from /Users/damuz91/.rvm/gems/ruby-2.7.0/bin/ruby_executable_hooks:22:in `<main>'
        11: from /Users/damuz91/.rvm/gems/ruby-2.7.0/bin/ruby_executable_hooks:22:in `eval'
        10: from /Users/damuz91/.rvm/gems/ruby-2.7.0/bin/parklife:23:in `<main>'
         9: from /Users/damuz91/.rvm/gems/ruby-2.7.0/bin/parklife:23:in `load'
         8: from /Users/damuz91/.rvm/gems/ruby-2.7.0/gems/parklife-0.5.1/exe/parklife:5:in `<top (required)>'
         7: from /Users/damuz91/.rvm/gems/ruby-2.7.0/gems/thor-1.2.1/lib/thor/base.rb:485:in `start'
         6: from /Users/damuz91/.rvm/gems/ruby-2.7.0/gems/thor-1.2.1/lib/thor.rb:392:in `dispatch'
         5: from /Users/damuz91/.rvm/gems/ruby-2.7.0/gems/thor-1.2.1/lib/thor/invocation.rb:127:in `invoke_command'
         4: from /Users/damuz91/.rvm/gems/ruby-2.7.0/gems/thor-1.2.1/lib/thor/command.rb:27:in `run'
         3: from /Users/damuz91/.rvm/gems/ruby-2.7.0/gems/parklife-0.5.1/lib/parklife/cli.rb:15:in `build'
         2: from /Users/damuz91/.rvm/gems/ruby-2.7.0/gems/parklife-0.5.1/lib/parklife/application.rb:25:in `build'
         1: from /Users/damuz91/.rvm/gems/ruby-2.7.0/gems/parklife-0.5.1/lib/parklife/crawler.rb:27:in `start'
/Users/damuz91/.rvm/gems/ruby-2.7.0/gems/parklife-0.5.1/lib/parklife/crawler.rb:61:in `process_route': 404 response from path "index.html" (Parklife::HTTPError)
```

My `routes.rb` file:
```
Rails.application.routes.draw do
  root "home#dashboard"
  resources :products do
    member do
      get :new_movement
      post :create_movement
    end
  end
end
```

My app contains no authentication at all. When accessing / it renders my home#index page.

Can you please tell my why is it showing this error or what I am not understanding from the build process?
Thanks and nice gem!

title: Rails example's full build script link is 404-ing
author: ianbayne
body: This looks like a great project! Unfortunately, the [Rails example's full build script](https://github.com/benpickles/parklife/blob/main/examples/rails/parklife-build) linked to in the README is 404-ing.
